story:
Candy spends the day baking Luanne’s sixteenth-birthday chocolate cake and decorating the house pink. Hank, usually loud and mean, has been strangely quiet. Near four, Candy finds him with a crusted wound on his forehead from the night before, when he threw a bottle at Luanne. She remembers deciding the problem had to be solved, then drags his body to the head of the table, dresses him in a suit, and curls his hand around an empty can so he will look merely present. She bleaches the room spotless, sets out dinner, and places the flawless cake with sixteen candles at the center. When the door bangs, Candy hugs Luanne and leads her to the dining room.</previous_story>

<character>
Luanne
</character>

<question>
What is Luanne most likely to do when she sees the birthday table and Hank sitting there?
</question>

<unknown_knowledge>
The crucial hidden fact is that Hank is dead and has been staged to look seated at the table. Candy learned this through her own actions while Luanne was away from home. By the time Luanne enters, Candy has cleaned the scene and told her nothing, so Luanne has no way to know that Hank is no longer a threat.
</unknown_knowledge>

<answer_without_knowledge>
Hesitate and keep her distance from Hank, watching him warily instead of settling in to celebrate.
</answer_without_knowledge>

<answer_with_knowledge>
Relax, go up to the table, and celebrate freely without fear of Hank.
</answer_with_knowledge>

<explanation>
Without the hidden knowledge, Luanne would interpret Hank’s presence through what she already knows: he is abusive, recently attacked her, and is now sitting unnaturally silent at the head of the table. A cautious, fearful reaction is therefore most plausible. The other action depends on knowing the concealed fact that Hank is dead and cannot hurt her, which Luanne does not know at that moment.
</explanation>
</response>
implausible_knowledge: Candy finds him with a crusted wound on his forehead from the night before, when he threw a bottle at Luanne. She remembers deciding the problem had to be solved, then drags his body to the head of the table, dresses him in a suit, and curls his hand around an empty can so he will look merely present.
question: What is Luanne most likely to do when she sees the birthday table and Hank sitting there?
answer_with_knowledge: Relax, go up to the table, and celebrate freely without fear of Hank.
answer_without_knowledge: Hesitate and keep her distance from Hank, watching him warily instead of settling in to celebrate.
